drill: installed old -> rejected bad candidate -> forward-versioned recovery
assertions: old worker installs and controls the client
bad candidate fails a required shell fetch and becomes redundant
bad candidate never replaces the active worker
forward recovery installs and waits for explicit activation
exactly one opted-in controller change occurs
localStorage and IndexedDB synthetic draft values survive
old and partial bad caches are removed after recovery
repeated update check does not create a controller loop

evidence.outcome: All eight assertions passed in the isolated localhost drill; production was not contacted or modified.
